CREATE TABLE test_table (col1 VARCHAR(10), col2 VARCHAR(10));
CREATE TABLE other_table (col3 VARCHAR(10), col4 VARCHAR(10));
SELECT * FROM test_table t JOIN other_table o ON "="(t.col1, o.col3) WHERE t.col1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM test_table t WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM other_table o WHERE "="(t.col1, o.col4));
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE "="(col1, col2) AND "="(col1, 'aa');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE NOT "="(col1, 'ab');
CREATE TABLE final_table (id INT, ref_col VARCHAR(10));
SELECT f.id FROM final_table f WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_table t WHERE "="(f.ref_col, t.col1) AND "="(f.ref_col, 'aa'));
SELECT t.col1, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM other_table o WHERE "="(o.col4, t.col2)) AS count_matches FROM test_table t GROUP BY t.col1 HAVING count_matches > 0;
